1	# Semantic Version Bumper
2	# Parses version strings, classifies conventional commits, bumps versions,
3	# and generates changelog entries.
4	
5	import re
6	from dataclasses import dataclass
7	from enum import Enum
8	
9	
10	@dataclass
11	class SemVer:
12	    """Represents a semantic version (major.minor.patch)."""
13	    major: int
14	    minor: int
15	    patch: int
16	
17	    def __str__(self):
18	        return f"{self.major}.{self.minor}.{self.patch}"
19	
20	
21	class BumpType(Enum):
22	    """The type of version bump indicated by a commit."""
23	    MAJOR = "major"
24	    MINOR = "minor"
25	    PATCH = "patch"
26	
27	
28	@dataclass
29	class Commit:
30	    """A parsed conventional commit."""
31	    bump_type: BumpType
32	    type: str | None      # e.g. "feat", "fix", "chore"
33	    description: str
34	    scope: str | None
35	
36	
37	# Maps conventional commit types to bump levels
38	_BUMP_MAP = {"feat": BumpType.MINOR}
39	# Everything else (fix, chore, docs, refactor, etc.) defaults to PATCH
40	
41	
42	def parse_commit(message: str) -> Commit:
43	    """Parse a conventional commit message and determine the bump type.
44	
45	    Supports: type[(scope)][!]: description
46	    A trailing '!' or 'BREAKING CHANGE:' footer triggers a major bump.
47	    """
48	    first_line = message.split("\n")[0]

import pytest

    read_version_file, write_version_file, generate_changelog,
)


class TestParseVersion:
    def test_parse_basic_version(self):
        v = parse_version("1.2.3")
        assert v == SemVer(1, 2, 3)

    def test_parse_zero_version(self):
        v = parse_version("0.0.0")
        assert v == SemVer(0, 0, 0)

    def test_parse_large_numbers(self):
        v = parse_version("10.200.3000")
        assert v == SemVer(10, 200, 3000)

    def test_parse_with_v_prefix(self):
        v = parse_version("v1.2.3")
        assert v == SemVer(1, 2, 3)

    def test_parse_invalid_version_raises(self):
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Invalid semantic version"):
            parse_version("not-a-version")

    def test_parse_incomplete_version_raises(self):
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Invalid semantic version"):
            parse_version("1.2")

    def test_semver_to_string(self):
        assert str(SemVer(1, 2, 3)) == "1.2.3"


# TDD Cycle 2: Parse conventional commit messages

class TestParseCommit:
    def test_feat_commit(self):
        c = parse_commit("feat: add user login")
        assert c == Commit(BumpType.MINOR, "feat", "add user login", None)

    def test_fix_commit(self):
        c = parse_commit("fix: handle null pointer")
        assert c == Commit(BumpType.PATCH, "fix", "handle null pointer", None)

    def test_feat_with_scope(self):
        c = parse_commit("feat(auth): add OAuth support")
        assert c == Commit(BumpType.MINOR, "feat", "add OAuth support", "auth")

    def test_fix_with_scope(self):
        c = parse_commit("fix(db): close idle connections")
        assert c == Commit(BumpType.PATCH, "fix", "close idle connections", "db")

    def test_breaking_change_with_bang(self):
        c = parse_commit("feat!: remove deprecated API")
        assert c.bump_type == BumpType.MAJOR

    def test_breaking_change_with_scope_and_bang(self):
        c = parse_commit("refactor(core)!: rewrite engine")
        assert c.bump_type == BumpType.MAJOR

    def test_chore_commit(self):
        c = parse_commit("chore: update deps")
        assert c == Commit(BumpType.PATCH, "chore", "update deps", None)

    def test_docs_commit(self):
        c = parse_commit("docs: update readme")
        assert c == Commit(BumpType.PATCH, "docs", "update readme", None)

    def test_non_conventional_commit_returns_patch(self):
        c = parse_commit("just a regular commit message")
        assert c == Commit(BumpType.PATCH, None, "just a regular commit message", None)

1	# Semantic Version Bumper
2	# Parses version strings, classifies conventional commits, bumps versions,
3	# and generates changelog entries.
4	
5	import json
6	import re
7	from dataclasses import dataclass
8	from enum import Enum
9	from pathlib import Path
10	
11	
12	@dataclass
13	class SemVer:
14	    """Represents a semantic version (major.minor.patch)."""
15	    major: int
16	    minor: int
17	    patch: int
18	
19	    def __str__(self):
20	        return f"{self.major}.{self.minor}.{self.patch}"
21	
22	
23	class BumpType(Enum):
24	    """The type of version bump indicated by a commit."""
25	    MAJOR = "major"
26	    MINOR = "minor"
27	    PATCH = "patch"
28	
29	
30	@dataclass
31	class Commit:
32	    """A parsed conventional commit."""
33	    bump_type: BumpType
34	    type: str | None      # e.g. "feat", "fix", "chore"
35	    description: str
36	    scope: str | None
37	
38	
39	# Maps conventional commit types to bump levels
40	_BUMP_MAP = {"feat": BumpType.MINOR}
41	# Everything else (fix, chore, docs, refactor, etc.) defaults to PATCH
42	
43	
44	def parse_commit(message: str) -> Commit:
45	    """Parse a conventional commit message and determine the bump type.
46	
47	    Supports: type[(scope)][!]: description
48	    A trailing '!' or 'BREAKING CHANGE:' footer triggers a major bump.
49	    """
50	    first_line = message.split("\n")[0]
51	    is_breaking = "BREAKING CHANGE:" in message or "BREAKING-CHANGE:" in message
52	
53	    # Match conventional commit pattern: type(scope)!: description
54	    match = re.match(r"^(\w+)(?:\(([^)]+)\))?(!)?\s*:\s*(.+)$", first_line)
55	    if not match:
56	        # Non-conventional commit — treat as patch
57	        return Commit(BumpType.PATCH, None, first_line.strip(), None)
58	
59	    ctype, scope, bang, desc = match.groups()
60	    if bang or is_breaking:
61	        bump = BumpType.MAJOR
62	    else:
63	        bump = _BUMP_MAP.get(ctype, BumpType.PATCH)
64	
65	    return Commit(bump, ctype, desc.strip(), scope)
66	
67	
68	def bump_version(current: SemVer, commit_messages: list[str]) -> SemVer:
69	    """Determine the next version by analyzing commit messages.
70	
71	    The highest bump type wins: major > minor > patch.
72	    Major resets minor+patch, minor resets patch.
73	    """
74	    if not commit_messages:
75	        raise ValueError("No commits provided — cannot determine version bump")
76	
77	    # Parse all commits and find the highest bump level
78	    # MAJOR > MINOR > PATCH, so we use ordering: MAJOR=0, MINOR=1, PATCH=2
79	    priority = {BumpType.MAJOR: 0, BumpType.MINOR: 1, BumpType.PATCH: 2}
80	    parsed = [parse_commit(msg) for msg in commit_messages]
81	    highest = min(parsed, key=lambda c: priority[c.bump_type]).bump_type
82	
83	    if highest == BumpType.MAJOR:
84	        return SemVer(current.major + 1, 0, 0)
85	    elif highest == BumpType.MINOR:
86	        return SemVer(current.major, current.minor + 1, 0)
87	    else:
88	        return SemVer(current.major, current.minor, current.patch + 1)
89	
90	
91	def parse_version(version_str: str) -> SemVer:
92	    """Parse a semantic version string like '1.2.3' or 'v1.2.3'."""
93	    match = re.match(r"^v?(\d+)\.(\d+)\.(\d+)$", version_str.strip())
94	    if not match:
95	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid semantic version: '{version_str}'")
96	    return SemVer(int(match.group(1)), int(match.group(2)), int(match.group(3)))
97	
98	
99	def read_version_file(path: str) -> SemVer:
100	    """Read a version from a VERSION file or package.json."""
101	    p = Path(path)
102	    if not p.exists():
103	        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Version file not found: {path}")
104	
105	    content = p.read_text()
106	
107	    if p.name == "package.json":
108	        data = json.loads(content)
109	        if "version" not in data:
110	            raise ValueError(f"No 'version' field in {path}")
111	        return parse_version(data["version"])
112	
113	    # Plain text version file (e.g. VERSION)
114	    return parse_version(content.strip())
115	
116	
117	def write_version_file(path: str, version: SemVer) -> None:
118	    """Write the new version to a VERSION file or package.json."""
119	    p = Path(path)
120	
121	    if p.name == "package.json":
122	        data = json.loads(p.read_text())
123	        data["version"] = str(version)
124	        p.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2) + "\n")
125	    else:
126	        p.write_text(str(version) + "\n")
127	
128	
129	# Changelog section headers mapped from commit types
130	_SECTION_MAP = {
131	    "feat": "Features",
132	    "fix": "Bug Fixes",
133	}
134	
135	
136	def generate_changelog(version: SemVer, commit_messages: list[str]) -> str:
137	    """Generate a markdown changelog entry grouped by commit type."""
138	    if not commit_messages:
139	        raise ValueError("No commits provided — cannot generate changelog")
140	
141	    parsed = [parse_commit(msg) for msg in commit_messages]
142	
143	    # Group commits by section
144	    sections: dict[str, list[Commit]] = {}
145	    for c in parsed:
146	        section = _SECTION_MAP.get(c.type, "Other") if c.type else "Other"
147	        sections.setdefault(section, []).append(c)
148	
149	    lines = [f"## {version}\n"]
150	    # Render known sections first in a stable order, then "Other"
151	    for section in ["Features", "Bug Fixes", "Other"]:
152	        if section not in sections:
153	            continue
154	        lines.append(f"### {section}\n")
155	        for c in sections[section]:
156	            prefix = "**BREAKING** " if c.bump_type == BumpType.MAJOR else ""
157	            scope = f"**{c.scope}**: " if c.scope else ""
158	            lines.append(f"- {prefix}{scope}{c.description}")
159	        lines.append("")
160	
161	    return "\n".join(lines).rstrip() + "\n"
